content.theorem: A fixed-width 4-byte bytecode encoding enables single-pass compilation and cache-friendly interpretation
content.proof: Format: [opcode: 1 byte][operand_a: 1 byte][operand_b: 1 byte][operand_c: 1 byte]. Fixed width means: no decoding step, no variable-length parsing, direct array indexing for program counter. All 247 opcodes fit in 1 byte. Operands index into constant pool or register file. Cache line (64 bytes) holds 16 instructions.
content.benchmark: Bytecode size for snap function: 48 bytes (12 instructions). Dialog evaluation: 200 bytes (50 instructions). Full room: ~2KB. Entire PLATO: ~50KB.
content.caveat: Fixed 4-byte format wastes space for opcodes with <3 operands (wasted bytes). Variable-length encoding would save ~30% but complicate the interpreter.
